text:
The change is done, but my verification run was blocked — executing `node` needs your approval, which I don't have, so I can't claim the tests pass yet.

Here's the one-line fix I made in `flatten.js`:

```js
if (Array.isArray(x)) for (const y of flatten(x)) out.push(y);
```

Previously the inner loop copied an array element's contents verbatim (one level only); now it recurses with `flatten(x)`, so each nested array is fully flattened before its values are appended. This handles arbitrary depth — `[1, [2, [3, [4]]]]` → `[1, 2, 3, 4]` — and empty nested arrays like `[[[]]]` collapse to `[]`. Non-array elements still pass through unchanged.

If you approve the `node` command (or tell me how you'd like tests run), I'll run the deep-case checks and confirm they pass.
